message:
So, there I was, cozy at home during a winter storm, with snow piling up outside and my phone buzzing with alerts. I thought I was all set, but then my phone battery dipped dangerously low, and the power flickered. Talk about a panic moment! I scrambled to find my portable charger, but it was buried under a pile of blankets and snacks (priorities, right?).

In that moment, I realized how crucial it is to have a backup plan for our tech. A simple power bank or even a good old-fashioned battery-operated radio can be a lifesaver when the grid goes down. 

I’m grateful I found my charger just in time, and I’ve since made it a point to keep my tech essentials organized and ready for anything. 

Have you ever had a tech scare during a storm? How do you prep to stay connected when the weather gets wild?
